terms.title: Appliqué
terms.description: A decorative technique where pieces of fabric are sewn onto another fabric to create patterns or designs

terms.title: Back Stitch
terms.description: A strong hand stitch where each stitch goes slightly backward, creating a solid, continuous line often used for seams

terms.title: Back Tack
terms.description: Sewing backward for a few stitches to lock the thread and prevent seams from coming undone

terms.title: Bias Binding
terms.description: Strips of fabric cut diagonally, used to neatly finish edges and curves

terms.title: Bobbin
terms.description: A small spool under the needle plate that holds the lower thread in a sewing machine

terms.title: Grain
terms.description: The direction of threads in fabric, including lengthwise and crosswise lines

terms.title: Interfacing
terms.description: An extra layer of fabric added inside to give shape and support to garments

terms.title: Presser Foot
terms.description: The part of the sewing machine that holds the fabric in place while stitching

terms.title: Raw Edge
terms.description: The unfinished edge of fabric that can fray if not sewn or finished

terms.title: Reverse Stitch
terms.description: A machine function that sews backward to secure stitches at the beginning or end

terms.title: Right Side
terms.description: The outer, visible side of the fabric when the project is finished

terms.title: Seam Allowance
terms.description: The extra fabric between the stitching line and the edge, used to join pieces together

terms.title: Self-Lined Fabric
terms.description: Using the same fabric on both the inside and outside of a garment

terms.title: Selvedge
terms.description: The finished edge of fabric that doesn’t fray and runs along its length

terms.title: Stay Stitch
terms.description: A line of stitching sewn close to the edge to keep fabric from stretching out of shape

terms.title: Tacking Stitch
terms.description: Temporary hand stitches used to hold fabric pieces in place before final sewing

terms.title: Top Stitch
terms.description: A visible line of stitching on the outside of a garment, often used for decoration or strength

terms.title: Warp
terms.description: The threads that run lengthwise in fabric and stay fixed on the loom

terms.title: Weft
terms.description: The threads woven across the fabric, going over and under the warp threads

terms.title: Wrong Side
terms.description: The inside or hidden side of the fabric

terms.title: Zig-Zag Stitch
terms.description: A stitch that moves side to side, used to prevent fraying or add decoration
